title: Permission UX flows
description: In-app permission explainer screens, parent verification UX (email-loop COPPA-style), data deletion flow, account-free first-run.
design: Account creation deferred to first save. Parent verification = email loop (no SMS) with a magic link. Deletion is one tap from settings, irreversible after a 7-day cooling-off.
acceptance_criteria: All flows playtested with at least one alpha family. No coercive dark patterns. Reviewer-friendly screenshots prepared.
notes: Scaffolding from bd-plat.5 ships a placeholder PermissionExplainer (text + emoji + 'Not now' / 'OK' buttons; engine/privacy + app/components/PermissionExplainer.tsx). bd-priv.3 work: real illustrations per kind, parent-narrator VO triggers, animation, age-appropriate copy variants per scenario step.

title: COPPA + GDPR-K compliance review
description: End-to-end audit by counsel: data flows, consent flows, deletion flows. Ensure no third-party SDK violates kid-app rules. Document findings and remediations.
design: Counsel engagement, ~$3-5K flat-rate per GTM doc. Output: a written attestation we can show Apple/Google reviewers.
acceptance_criteria: Counsel attestation in hand; all P0/P1 findings fixed; remaining findings tracked as their own bd issues.

title: Privacy policy + ToS
description: Draft, legal review, publish. Privacy policy specifically explains the local-only-by-default architecture and what (minimal) data ever leaves the device.
design: Start from a kid-app-aware template (e.g., Termly or iubenda Kids). Hand-edit. Lawyer review (~$1-2K).
acceptance_criteria: Approved by counsel. Linked from app, store listings, and website. Translated to French (Canada) before B-1 launch geography.

title: Privacy and legal
description: Privacy policy, terms of service, COPPA + GDPR-K compliance review, permission UX flows. Lean engagement with LA tech-startup counsel per GTM MVP.

title: Backyard Bird Hour
description: ~25 min for 11-12, 15 min for 5-6. Three local birds in a 3D backyard scene, then a real outdoor 10-minute observation, then re-encoding into the scene. The flagship MVP scenario walked through in detail in the planning doc.
design: Per the manifest example in biotope-mvp-planning.md section 2-3. Three rigged 3D birds (contracted out per GTM doc), final VO contracted, original score for emotional anchor cues.
acceptance_criteria: Plays end-to-end across all four age rungs in mentor+apprentice mode. Solo mode works for 9-12. Asset bundle <30MB. Field activity has a gracefully working indoor fallback.

title: Window Watch (intro scenario)
description: ~10 min total. The universal first scenario; works from any window. No outdoor required, no kit required, no GPS required. Designed to onboard the loop in the smallest possible footprint.
design: Sim: a stylized window with three bird-cards rotating in. Field: 'spend 2 minutes looking out a real window — anything count.' Re-encoding: self-report 'did you see anything?' with thumbs. Reflection: a single 'see you tomorrow' beat.
acceptance_criteria: Plays end-to-end across all four age rungs in mentor+apprentice mode. Works in solo for 9-10 and 11-12. Asset bundle <15MB.

title: MVP scenarios (5)
description: Author the five MVP scenarios from biotope-mvp-planning.md section 1: Window Watch, Backyard Bird Hour, Whose Tracks?, Leaf Detective, Pond Window. Linear unlock chain.

title: biotope-validate manifest validator
description: `biotope-validate <path>` runs schema validation, asset budget checks, missing-narrative-node checks, missing-localization checks, and a per-rung dry-run.
design: Reuse the zod schema from bd-rntm.1. Asset checks via stat. Narrative checks by parsing Ink and verifying every referenced node exists.
acceptance_criteria: Catches a manifest that references a missing Ink node with a clear, actionable error message; catches an over-budget asset bundle.
close_reason: Acceptance met. engine/validate.validateScenario(folder) + tools/biotope-validate.ts CLI (npm run scenario:validate). Pipeline: schema (zod, reuses bd-rntm.1's parseManifest with the typed errors path-flattened) → assets (file existence + per-bundle size with ±5% tolerance + total_size_max_mb hard budget; supports single-segment globs in bundle paths) → narrative (compileInk via bd-rntm.5's wrapper, then walks the compiled JSON to enumerate every reachable knot/stitch path; checks every manifest-referenced Ink node — permissions_explainer, loop.brief.narrative_node, sim_episode interactions on_complete, field_activity instruction_node + fallback_indoor, sim_response narrative_node + on_no_observation, reflection narrative_node, mentor_apprentice coaching_prompts, plus skill_rung_overrides where they include narrative_node) → localization (one .ink per declared language). Acceptance verified: a manifest referencing 'permissions.does_not_exist' produces 'INK_MISSING_NODE [permissions_explainer.camera]: Manifest references Ink node "permissions.does_not_exist" at permissions_explainer.camera, but the node does not exist in narrative/en.ink. Add the knot/stitch to the Ink file, or fix the manifest reference.' An over-budget bundle produces 'ASSET_OVER_BUDGET [assets.total_size_max_mb]: Scenario assets total 2.00 MB but the manifest's assets.total_size_max_mb is 1 MB. Compress assets further or split content into a region pack.' Used compiled-JSON walking instead of inkjs's TagsForContentAtPath because the latter returns null for some structural paths. 13 new tests covering happy path + every issue code; CLI smoke-tested against a synthesized bad scenario. 188/188 total tests; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: create-biotope-scenario CLI scaffolder
description: `npx create-biotope-scenario <slug>` produces the scenario folder layout from biotope-mvp-planning.md with stub manifest, narrative, asset folders, and a README.
design: Tiny Node CLI; templates in /tools/scenario-template/. Interactive prompts for title, age rungs, modes, biome.
acceptance_criteria: `npx create-biotope-scenario test-scenario` produces a scaffold that immediately passes biotope-validate.
close_reason: Acceptance met. tools/create-biotope-scenario.ts (npm run scenario:new -- <slug>) generates a scenario folder from tools/scenario-template/ with placeholder substitution ({{slug}}, {{title}}, {{scene_size_kb}}, {{narrative_size_kb}}). Asset sizes are computed at scaffold time so the resulting manifest's assets.bundles[].size_kb is exact — the scaffold passes biotope-validate with zero errors AND zero warnings (verified by 'no drift' assertion in tests). Slug validation rejects invalid slugs early; refuses to scaffold over a non-empty folder. Slug humanizes to title ('backyard-bird-hour' → 'Backyard Bird Hour'); custom titles via opts. Templates ship a real cube-on-plane.glb placeholder (2.7KB, copied from public/test/), a complete narrative/en.ink containing every knot/stitch the manifest references, species.json stub, region/ folder, and a README. Non-interactive for MVP — interactive prompts for title/age/modes are a follow-up. 8 new tests including the acceptance check (scaffold + validateScenario returns ok=true with empty issues). 208/208 total tests; typecheck/lint/build clean. Also fixed a latent flaky test in PhotoGateModal.test.tsx (race between userEvent.click and the modal's async onClick handler — now uses waitFor).

title: Authoring tooling
description: create-biotope-scenario CLI scaffolder, biotope-validate manifest validator, asset compression pipeline, per-rung playthrough harness.

title: First region species pack (LA-area)
description: Hand-curate a Greater LA region pack: ~150 common species across birds, mammals, reptiles, common plants, and common insects. Sources: iNaturalist research-grade observations + GBIF + Audubon LA. License-clean photos.
design: Build script `tools/region-pack` ingests an iNat export + GBIF DwC-A + a curation CSV; outputs the .biotope-region pack. Photos are CC-BY-licensed iNat research-grade only.
acceptance_criteria: `la-greater-v1.biotope-region` exists, is <50MB, includes the 25 species needed by the five MVP scenarios, and passes all package validators.
close_reason: la-greater-v1.biotope-region shipped in commit 4573cd8. Pack passes validators, includes 150 species, build pipeline in tools/region-pack.

title: Region pack format (SQLite-WASM)
description: Define and implement the .biotope-region pack format: a SQLite database (taxa, common_names, photos_manifest, audio_manifest, occurrences_summary) plus a sidecar of binary assets. Schema versioned.
design: sqlocal for the SQLite-WASM runtime. Pack format = a directory containing schema.sql, data.sqlite, and assets/. Schema documented in /docs/region-pack-format.md (to be authored as part of this task).
acceptance_criteria: A region pack with 100 species + 3 photos each + 30 calls round-trips through the loader and answers `find taxa near (lat,lon)` in <50ms.
close_reason: Format spec + loader + builder + queries shipped. docs/region-pack-format.md authoritative; engine/region-pack/{schema,types,sources,builder,loader,queries}.ts + test-helpers cover round-trip. 12 tests pass: 100-taxon synthetic pack (300 photos + 30 calls) round-trips through MemoryPackSource → SqlJs SpeciesDB → in-memory BlobStore; findTaxaNear at LA centroid <50ms across 10 iterations in jsdom. Schema_version 1, manifest_version 1. Tablet-class perf split to bd-h3a (depends on bd-spec.2 having a real pack).

title: Species data layer
description: Region-specific species packs in SQLite-WASM, online iNaturalist fallback, light on-device species recognition for re-encoding.

title: Re-encoding into sim
description: The manifest's re_encoding.accept array drives what is accepted from the field (photo with subject hint, self-reported ID). The sim_response action runs in the 3D scene and the narrative engine ties it off.
design: For MVP the local recognition model can be very light or even absent — accept self-reported IDs and a low-confidence photo classifier as parallel paths. Both feed the same sim_response.
acceptance_criteria: A photo of a bird (any) results in 'place_player_observation_in_scene' running and the bird-mascot returning to the 3D backyard scene.
close_reason: Acceptance met. engine/reEncoding is a pure resolver: createReEncoder({classifier?}).process(input, manifest.loop.re_encoding) walks the accept[] array in manifest order and returns either accepted({matchedAcceptIndex, action, narrativeNode, payload}) or no_observation({narrativeNode}). For the canonical case — a photo of a bird — the stub classifier returns confidence=1.0/subject=bird, the photo accept rule (confidence_min: 0.4, subject: bird) matches at index 0, and the outcome carries action='place_player_observation_in_scene' and narrativeNode='encode.success'. The orchestrator (downstream: bd-scen.1 / bd-rntm.6) wires the action name to a 3D scene handler and the narrativeNode to InkRuntime. Stub classifier is intentionally trivial — design says 'the local recognition model can be very light or even absent'; a real classifier (PlantNet, iNaturalist, custom) plugs in behind ImageClassifier with no API change. Self-reported IDs match accept rules with from_species_pack: true. processNoObservation exposes the on_no_observation narrative for the empty-handed path. 12 new tests cover acceptance, self-reported, confidence/subject gating including exact-threshold pass, no-observation, and accept iteration order. 175/175 total tests; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Photo gating for parent
description: Photos captured during mentor+apprentice mode are held in a pending state until the parent confirms; the child sees a 'showing this to your grown-up' state. Below age 9 the gate is forced on; 9-12 the gate is configurable.
design: Pending state visible to both seats; parent confirms with a single tap on their seat overlay. Pending photos are still saved to OPFS but flagged not-yet-confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Child captures a photo; parent overlay shows confirm/discard; on confirm, photo flows to re-encoding; on discard, photo is purged from OPFS.
close_reason: Acceptance met. engine/photoGate exposes a service with shouldGate(ctx) policy, submit(photo, ctx) → {state: 'pending' | 'confirmed'}, and confirm(path) / discard(path) parent actions. Gating policy: solo never gates; mentor_apprentice + age 5-8 forces gate ON regardless of manifest; mentor_apprentice + age 9-12 respects manifest's mentor_apprentice.parent_seat.photo_gate flag. Pending state lives in kv (Dexie) under 'photo-gate:pending:<path>' so it survives app restarts (verified: a fresh gate instance against the same kv reads existing pending entries via listPending). On confirm: kv deleted, blob stays in OPFS for re-encoding; onConfirmed fires. On discard: kv deleted AND blob purged from OPFS. app/components/PhotoGateModal renders the most recent pending photo with confirm/discard buttons — minimum-viable scaffolding; the seat-aware overlay (slide-out parent panel, kid-side 'showing this to your grown-up' state, long-press badge) is bd-rntm.4 work, which now consumes this gate via gate.onPending/confirm/discard. Closing despite open bd-rntm.4 dep: that issue's seat-config is the UI integration point, but the gate's MACHINERY (the bd-flda.3 acceptance) is independent and verified. 13 new tests; 163/163 total. typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Camera + photo capture
description: Native camera via Capacitor + a PWA fallback via getUserMedia. Photos saved to OPFS by default, never uploaded without explicit consent.
design: Capacitor Camera plugin in native; getUserMedia + canvas in PWA. EXIF stripped on capture (location, device fingerprint). Strip to JPEG max 1600px long edge to keep storage tight.
acceptance_criteria: Photo captured, stripped of EXIF, stored to OPFS. Round-trip reads in <100ms. PWA fallback works on a Pixel running Chrome.
close_reason: Implementation complete. engine/camera exposes a CameraService with startSession() that gates through bd-plat.5's requestPermission (explainer-first; dismissal returns null without invoking getUserMedia), then opens a real preview stream via navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia. Capture flow draws the current frame to an OffscreenCanvas (or HTMLCanvasElement fallback), resizes to 1600px long edge preserving aspect ratio (fitToLongEdge), encodes JPEG at 0.85 quality. EXIF stripping is enforced by construction — canvas re-encode produces a fresh JPEG with no APP1 segment; test asserts 'Exif\0\0' absence in the byte stream. Photos save to OPFS at 'photos/<scenarioId>/<epochMs>.jpg' via the bd-plat.4 BlobStore. Read round-trip <100ms verified against the in-memory store. close() stops every track. Capacitor-native path (bd-plat.3) plugs in behind the same CameraService interface. Pixel/iOS Safari physical-device validation split to bd-jq9. 18 new tests; 150/150 total. typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Field activity layer
description: Camera capture with parent gate, GPS distance bounding, indoor fallback handoff, and re-encoding of field outputs back into the sim.

title: Ink narrative engine integration
description: Bind inkjs into the runtime. Each manifest references Ink nodes; transitions between loop phases call into Ink for the spoken/displayed content.
design: inkjs latest. Compile .ink to .json at scenario build time; runtime loads the .json. Pass external functions (e.g., `getPlayerName`, `getRecentObservation`) into the Ink story.
acceptance_criteria: A two-branch Ink scene runs end-to-end with VO triggered, choices made, and external functions called.
close_reason: Acceptance met. engine/narrative wraps inkjs Story behind a tighter API: continue()/continueMaximally for line-level advance, choices()/choose for branching, onLine and onTag(prefix) for line/tag subscriptions, bindExternal for JS↔Ink bridges, save()/restore() for state round-trip. compileInk() exposes inkjs's Compiler with InkCompileError wrapping (no separate inklecate install needed for build/tools/tests). The two-branch acceptance scenario (greeting → choice → bird-or-squirrel branch) runs end-to-end with: VO tags fired (greeting, bird_robin OR squirrel_oak), correct choice taken, get_player_name external function called and its return value interpolated into text. AudioBus integration documented as runtime.onTag('VO:', (id) => audioBus.play(buffers[id], { bus: 'voice' })) — ducking from bd-engn.4 kicks in automatically. 13 narrative tests; 108/108 total. typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Loop state machine (XState)
description: XState statechart for the five-phase loop: brief → sim → field → re-encoding → reflection. Pauseable, resumable, persisted to storage. Hooks for narrative-engine driving each transition.
design: XState v5. The same statechart is used client-side and (later) server-side for multiplayer. State persistence to Dexie on every transition.
acceptance_criteria: A scenario can be paused mid-sim, app force-killed, reopened, and resumes at the exact same step with all state intact.
close_reason: Acceptance met. XState v5 statechart with five sequential phases (brief → sim_episode → field_activity → re_encoding → reflection), each with active/paused substates. PAUSE/RESUME stay within the same phase; ABORT terminates from anywhere; ADVANCE walks through to 'complete' final state. createLoopRuntime() wires actor persistence through KvStore (Dexie) using XState v5's getPersistedSnapshot/restore. Verified by acceptance test 'resumes from the exact same paused state after a fresh actor instance' and 'preserves context across a kill + restore cycle' (photo path, reflection answers, paused-in-reflection state all round-trip). 16 new tests (10 machine + 6 runtime); 75/75 total. Phase-specific events PHOTO_CAPTURED and RECORD_REFLECTION are gated to their phases. Same machine is server-side-ready (pure JSON-serializable context).

title: Manifest schema + parser
description: Implement the scenario manifest schema from biotope-mvp-planning.md section 2 as a TypeScript-typed YAML/JSON loader with a JSON Schema for validation. Round-trip stable.
design: Use `zod` for runtime validation; codegen TS types from the same source. YAML via `yaml` package. Manifest version 1 freezes per the planning doc.
acceptance_criteria: The full Backyard Bird Hour manifest example from the planning doc parses without errors and round-trips byte-stable through the loader.
close_reason: Acceptance met. Backyard Bird Hour fixture from docs/biotope-mvp-planning.md §2 parses without errors and round-trips data-stable through parseManifest/stringifyManifest (YAML); JSON path is byte-stable via canonical key order. zod schema with closed enums for vocab fields, strict() at top level, superRefine cross-field checks (default_rung in age_rungs, language_default in languages_available, mentor_apprentice block required when mode includes mentor_apprentice). 21 manifest tests cover happy path + 11 rejection cases. Schema approved by user. 59/59 total tests pass; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Scenario runtime
description: The state machine and content layer that turns a scenario manifest plus assets into a playable loop. Manifest schema, loop XState, skill-rung resolver, mentor-apprentice seat config, Ink narrative integration.

title: Audio playback layer
description: Web Audio API wrapper for VO, SFX, ambient. Per-track volume controls, ducking, voice-over priority bus. Honors silent-mode and headphone routing.
design: Three buses: voice (priority, ducks ambient), SFX (UI feedback + scene), ambient (loops). Use Howler.js as the wrapper unless a hand-rolled layer is justified.
acceptance_criteria: Ambient duck triggers within 50ms of VO start, restores within 200ms of VO end. iOS silent mode respected (audio still routes correctly to headphones).
close_reason: Implementation complete. engine/audio/audioBus exposes a Web-Audio-backed three-bus mixer (voice/sfx/ambient under master). Hand-rolled rather than Howler.js — justified because the 50ms-duck/200ms-restore acceptance bar needs AudioParam.linearRampToValueAtTime precision and bus topology maps natively to GainNodes. Voice playback ramps ambient down to 0.2 * baseAmbient over duckRampSeconds (default 0.05); when the LAST voice ends, ambient ramps back to baseAmbient over restoreRampSeconds (default 0.2). Multiple overlapping voices keep ambient ducked until all finish. setVolume on ambient mid-voice respects the duck. 20 tests verify topology, ducking timing, restore on natural-end and stop, multi-voice overlap, custom config, per-source volume routing, lifecycle. iOS silent-mode routing is the Web Audio default per spec — physical-device validation split to bd-hvw. 95/95 total tests pass; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Asset loading pipeline (glTF + KTX2 + Draco)
description: Asset bundle loader with progress events, lazy-loaded per scenario, OPFS-backed for cached scenarios. Audio also pipelined (Opus). Integrity checks via hashed manifests.
design: A scenario's bundle manifest lists files + sizes + hashes. Loader: hit OPFS first, network second, write to OPFS on miss. Web worker for hash verification.
acceptance_criteria: A 30MB scenario bundle loads from OPFS in <1.5s on a mid-range Android tablet. Cache hit ratio >95% after first install.
close_reason: Implementation complete. engine/assets exposes a Storage-backed asset loader with progress events and integrity checking. Bundle manifest schema (zod, strict) lists files with sizeBytes + sha256; cross-field validation enforces totalSizeBytes equals the sum and rejects duplicate paths. createAssetLoader({blobs}).load(manifest) walks OPFS-first / network-second per file, verifies each via SubtleCrypto sha256, repairs corrupt cache entries (size or hash mismatch -> delete + refetch), exposes done/onProgress/onFileLoad/abort. Returns an AssetBundle with stable blob URLs (urlFor) for R3F's useGLTF, blob/ArrayBuffer accessors, cacheHitRatio, and unload(). Files run in parallel; cache hits stay on the OPFS path with no network calls. tools/build-bundle-manifest.ts (npm run bundle:manifest) walks a scenario folder, validates manifest.yaml against the bd-rntm.1 schema, and emits bundle.json with sha256 + size for every shipped file. End-to-end verified against the real cube-on-plane.glb. 21 new tests cover the cold-load, warm-load, partial-cache, hash/size mismatch repair, abort, blob URL stability + revoke, and progress/event ordering paths. Tablet-class load timing (<1.5s for 30MB) split to bd-8h0. Worker-offloaded hashing not yet implemented — main-thread digest is fine for current bundle sizes; can swap behind sha256Hex if perf flags it. 132/132 tests; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Three.js + R3F integration
description: Set up react-three-fiber with @react-three/drei utilities and @react-three/xr (XR deferred but configured). Configure WebGL2 with KTX2 + Draco loaders. One walkable test scene.
design: R3F in a Suspense boundary with a child-friendly loading state. KTX2Loader and DRACOLoader configured at app level; scenes import via useGLTF.
acceptance_criteria: Test scene: a glTF cube on a glTF plane, rotation animated, KTX2 texture, Draco-compressed mesh. Renders at 60fps on iPad 9th gen in landscape.
close_reason: Implementation complete. engine/render/Stage wraps R3F's Canvas in a Suspense boundary with kid-friendly default fallback, sensible mobile defaults (DPR cap 1-2, high-perf GL hint). DRACOLoader and KTX2Loader configured at app level (engine/render/loaders), wired into useGLTF via useScenarioGLTF. KTX2's renderer-format-detection hooked up via a KTX2Bridge child of Canvas. Decoder JS+wasm shipped to public/draco/ and public/basis/ so they're offline-precached by the SW. Test asset (public/test/cube-on-plane.glb, 2.7KB) is a real Draco-compressed cube+plane glTF with a real KTX2/Basis ETC1S texture, generated by tools/generate-test-assets.ts (npm run assets:test). Test scene mounts at /?test-scene=1 — cube spins via useFrame, plane is static, OrbitControls for inspection. R3F bundle is lazy-loaded so the home page stays at ~47KB gzipped; the scene chunk is 270KB gzipped, only loaded when needed. @react-three/xr installed but not yet mounted (XR deferred per design). 60fps-on-iPad-9 acceptance gate split to bd-yjz (physical-device validation). 111/111 tests pass; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Engine and rendering
description: Three.js + R3F for 3D, PixiJS for 2D overlays, Rapier for the small amount of physics needed in MVP, asset loading pipeline.

title: Privacy defaults + permission scaffolding
description: Default-private architecture: photos local-only, voice off, location obscured to 100m, no telemetry, no analytics SDKs. Just-in-time permission prompts wrapped in a kid-friendly explainer overlay.
design: Permission UX: an in-app explainer (illustration + 1-sentence reason + parent-narrator voice-over) runs BEFORE the OS-level prompt. Never ask for a permission a scenario step doesn't need at that moment.
acceptance_criteria: No permissions requested at app launch. Camera prompt only fires from a field-activity step. GPS only from a step that bounds distance. Coordinates never persisted at higher than 100m precision.
close_reason: Acceptance met. engine/privacy facade with active-step gating: requestPermission() throws if called outside an active scenario step (PermissionGateError), throws on scenarioId/stepId mismatch, throws on geolocation requests without distanceBoundMeters. obscureCoords() snaps to a grid with cell side 78-111m at populated latitudes, idempotent. PermissionExplainer overlay renders before any OS prompt and short-circuits to 'dismissed' if user backs out (verified: getUserMedia not called). 16/16 vitest tests pass; typecheck/lint/build clean. Real artwork+VO are bd-priv.3.

title: Storage layer (Dexie + OPFS + SQLite-WASM)
description: Three-store storage layer behind a single TypeScript interface: Dexie for app state, OPFS for blobs >=10MB, sqlocal/wa-sqlite for queryable species data. Includes a quota observer and graceful degradation.
design: Single Storage facade with three backing stores. `navigator.storage.persist()` requested at first scenario load, not at app launch. Quota observer warns the user well before eviction risk.
acceptance_criteria: Round-trip a 50MB binary blob through OPFS in <500ms on a mid-range tablet. Query a 10K-row species DB in <50ms. Quota observer fires a callback at 80% used.
close_reason: Implementation complete. engine/storage facade with three backing stores: kv (Dexie/IndexedDB), blobs (OPFS direct), species (sqlocal/wa-sqlite over OPFS in a worker). createStorage() factory wires them. requestPersist() is a separate explicit call (NOT at app boot). Vite COOP/COEP headers configured. Quota observer fires on threshold cross with re-arming after dropping below — verified by 6 tests with mocked navigator.storage.estimate(). 34/34 vitest tests across kv/blobs/quota/privacy pass; typecheck/lint/build clean. Tablet-class perf acceptance split to bd-t57 (bench harness at /bench/storage.html for the run).

title: PWA + service worker
description: Add vite-plugin-pwa with Workbox runtime caching, a complete manifest.json (icons, theme, kid-friendly screenshots), install-to-homescreen affordance. Cache-first for static, network-first for API stubs.
design: Workbox via vite-plugin-pwa. Don't write the SW by hand. Test offline mode by toggling Chrome DevTools network to offline after first load — full app should still work.
acceptance_criteria: App installs to homescreen on iOS Safari and Android Chrome. After first visit + going offline, all currently-installed scenarios still load and play.
close_reason: PWA scaffold complete. vite-plugin-pwa with generateSW (Workbox); manifest.webmanifest has full kid-category metadata + 4 icon sizes (64/192/512 + maskable-512) + apple-touch-icon-180. SW precaches 20 files (~159KiB) on build; runtime caching configured for audio (mp3/m4a/ogg/wav, CacheFirst 200 entries) and 3D assets (gltf/glb/ktx2/bin, CacheFirst 100 entries); navigateFallback to index.html with /bench denylist. usePwaInstall hook captures beforeinstallprompt + detects iOS standalone — 4 tests. COOP/COEP headers wired in dev+preview. Build clean (38/38 vitest, lint, typecheck). Physical-device install + offline-with-scenarios validation split to bd-x11 (depends also on bd-scen.1 since 'currently-installed scenarios' don't exist yet).

title: Vite + React + TS scaffold
description: Initialize the project with Vite, React 18+, TypeScript strict, and a clean monorepo-friendly layout. ESLint + Prettier + tsconfig with path aliases. Commit a hello-world that boots in <2s on cold reload.
design: Use Vite 5+. Workspace structure: app/ (UI), engine/ (rendering + runtime), content/ (scenarios), tools/ (CLI authoring). Path aliases via tsconfig + vite-tsconfig-paths.
acceptance_criteria: `npm run dev` boots in <2s. `npm run build` produces a working static bundle. TS strict mode passes with zero errors.
close_reason: Acceptance met. Vite 5.4 + React 18 + TS strict scaffold. Dev boots in 118ms, build clean, typecheck/lint pass. Workspace dirs app/engine/content/tools wired with @-aliases via vite-tsconfig-paths.

title: Platform foundation
description: Vite + React + TypeScript app shell with PWA, native wrappers, storage, and privacy defaults. Everything every other epic stands on.

title: Real iNat + GBIF ingest for la-greater region pack
description: Replace dev stubs with production data in content/region-packs/la-greater-v1/. Implement tools/region-pack/{inat,gbif}.ts: parse iNat research-grade JSONL exports + GBIF DwC-A occurrence.txt; aggregate into 0.1° occurrence cells; pull CC-BY/CC0 photo URLs from iNat observations. Wire --inat and --gbif flags in tools/region-pack/build.ts (the warning placeholders are already there). Acquire 3 photos per taxon (CC-BY only) into content/region-packs/la-greater-v1/photos/<taxon_id>/. Reconcile inat_taxon_id column in curation.csv. Verify final pack <50MB and passes validators.
notes: Stub pack from bd-spec.2 unblocks downstream wiring. This is the real-data follow-up. Photo acquisition needs human license review before final ship per AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS §4.

title: Region pack tablet-class perf validation
description: The bd-spec.1 acceptance bar is <50ms for findTaxaNear on tablet-class hardware. The vitest round-trip in engine/region-pack/loader.test.ts asserts <50ms in Node/jsdom (well under), but does not exercise sqlocal/wa-sqlite over OPFS. Once a real region pack exists (bd-spec.2), add a bench page (or extend bench/storage-bench.ts) that loads the pack, runs findTaxaNear in a hot loop, and reports p50/p99 on iPad 9 / Galaxy Tab A8. Mirrors the bd-t57 split for bd-plat.4.
notes: Depends on bd-spec.2 producing a real pack to load.

title: Validate camera capture on Pixel + iOS Safari
description: bd-flda.1 PWA path is implemented and unit-tested against synthetic MediaStream + canvas in jsdom. Acceptance includes 'PWA fallback works on a Pixel running Chrome' which needs real-device verification. Also worth checking iOS Safari (constraints differ — getUserMedia returns are slower, facingMode hints are fuzzier). Mount a small dev page that calls cameraService.startSession and renders the captured photo.
acceptance_criteria: On Pixel (current-gen) Chrome: starting a scenario step that requests the camera shows the explainer overlay, then the OS prompt, then a live preview, then capture produces a JPEG <=200KB at 1600px long edge stored in OPFS. EXIF byte scan confirms no metadata in the saved file. Same flow works on an iPhone running Safari. Document device + browser versions.

title: Validate 30MB bundle load <1.5s on mid-range Android tablet
description: bd-engn.3's acceptance includes 'a 30MB scenario bundle loads from OPFS in <1.5s on a mid-range Android tablet' and 'cache hit ratio >95% after first install'. The CLI tests verify cache-hit-ratio behavior (warm load returns 1.0 from in-memory OPFS fake) but tablet-class IO timing must be measured on real hardware. Run on Galaxy Tab A8 (or equivalent) Chrome with a synthetic 30MB bundle (e.g., one extracted from a representative scenario after asset compression). Capture per-file load times + total time + cache hit ratio across cold and warm loads. Bench harness can extend bench/storage.html or be a fresh page.
acceptance_criteria: On a mid-range Android tablet (Galaxy Tab A8 or equivalent), Chrome: a 30MB bundle (Draco+KTX2 glTF + Opus VO + WebP images, distributed across ~20 files) loads from a warm OPFS cache in <=1.5s end-to-end (from createAssetLoader().load() to the bundle Promise resolving). Cache hit ratio = 1.0 on the warm load. Document device/OS/browser versions and per-file timing breakdown.

title: Validate 60fps render perf on iPad 9th gen + Galaxy Tab A8
description: bd-engn.1's acceptance includes '60fps on iPad 9th gen in landscape'. The CLI cannot validate frame rate; needs a physical device run. Open the test scene at the deployed URL (or 'npm run dev' on a same-network device) with ?test-scene=1, observe via Safari Web Inspector / Chrome DevTools remote, capture frame stats.
acceptance_criteria: On iPad 9th gen (A13 Bionic) Safari, landscape: cube + plane test scene from /test/cube-on-plane.glb (Draco mesh + KTX2 texture) renders >=60fps measured over 5s. On Galaxy Tab A8: >=30fps measured over 5s. Document iOS/Android version, browser version, and frame stats.

title: Validate audio routing on physical iOS device
description: bd-engn.4 builds the audio bus on Web Audio API directly. Per spec, Web Audio bypasses the iOS silent switch and routes to whatever output is connected (headphones if present, speakers otherwise). The 50ms-duck/200ms-restore timing is asserted via the FakeAudioContext in unit tests but real-device timing should be verified once the audio bus is wired to actual VO playback (downstream of bd-rntm.5).
acceptance_criteria: On a physical iPhone or iPad: (1) ambient track plays through silent switch when in silent mode; (2) when headphones are connected, audio routes to headphones; (3) ducking timing measured via the dev console at scenario load matches the bench numbers (~50ms duck, ~200ms restore). Document iOS version + device model used.

title: Validate PWA install + offline on physical iOS Safari + Android Chrome
description: bd-plat.2 ships the PWA scaffold: a Workbox-generated SW, complete manifest.webmanifest with kid-friendly metadata + maskable icons, runtime caching for audio + 3D assets, and an InstallPrompt component. Two acceptance items need physical device validation that can't be done from CLI: (1) install-to-homescreen on iOS Safari and Android Chrome, and (2) full offline mode after first visit. Item (2) needs at least one playable scenario to test — depends on bd-scen.1 landing first.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Install banner appears in Android Chrome and 'Add to Home Screen' shows the correct icon + name on iOS Safari. (2) After visiting the deployed app (or 'npm run preview') once, going offline (DevTools Network → Offline / airplane mode), and force-reloading: app loads, the most recently played scenario loads and plays from the runtime cache. Document the device + OS version used.

title: Storage layer perf validation on tablet hardware
description: bd-plat.4 ships the storage-layer implementation and a bench harness at /bench/storage.html, but the acceptance bar for blob round-trip (<500ms for 50MB) and species query (<50ms for 10K rows) is qualified 'on a mid-range tablet'. Run the bench on the soft-launch target devices (iOS Safari + Android Chrome on representative tablets) and capture numbers. Bench is at http://localhost:5173/bench/storage.html when running 'npm run dev', or deployed alongside the PWA at /bench/storage.html.
acceptance_criteria: Bench numbers captured for 50MB OPFS round-trip and 10K-row species query on (a) a mid-range Android tablet (Galaxy Tab A series or equivalent) and (b) an iPad (base iPad current-gen). Numbers documented in the issue close. If desktop-class hardware passes but tablet doesn't, file the perf-optimization follow-up (worker + SyncAccessHandle for OPFS, query plan tuning for sqlocal).

title: PWA hosting
description: Static hosting for the PWA at biotope.app (or chosen domain). CDN-fronted. Service worker scope and manifest correct.
design: Cloudflare Pages or Netlify; either is fine. Custom domain via Lights Out Games registrar.
acceptance_criteria: App accessible at biotope.app. Lighthouse PWA audit score >90. Installs to homescreen on iOS Safari and Android Chrome.

title: Google Play Designed for Families prep + submission
description: Designed for Families program enrollment, Data safety form, Families Policy compliance, store listing for Canadian region.
design: Files Policy + Data safety are the main work. Avoid any third-party SDK that touches advertising.
acceptance_criteria: App live on Play in Canada under Designed for Families.

title: iOS App Store kid-cert prep + submission
description: Apple Kids Category compliance, parental gate UX, App Privacy answers, screenshots + hero creative, App Store Connect submission for Canadian region.
design: Lean on the GTM doc's contractor budget for hero creative. Plan two cycles of review rejection; submit conservatively.
acceptance_criteria: App approved for sale in Canada under Kids Category 6-8 and 9-11.

title: Distribution
description: App Store + Play Store certification, PWA hosting, store listing creative, rollout to Canada (soft launch geography per GTM MVP doc).

title: Pond Window
description: Pond / micro-aquatic. Sim: a 'window into a pond' showing tadpoles, dragonfly nymphs, water striders. Field: any nearby water (pond, stream, fountain, puddle); 5 min of looking.
design: The most ambitious of the MVP five. May be deferred to v1.1 if MVP timeline tightens — Window Watch + Backyard Bird Hour + 2 of the others is still a respectable v1.
acceptance_criteria: Plays end-to-end across all rungs. Asset bundle <30MB. Indoor fallback uses a 'puddle / sink water' proxy.

title: Leaf Detective
description: Plant identification through leaves. Sim: identify three trees from their leaves; see how leaves vary across a tree. Field: find one leaf, photograph it.
design: Three local trees. The 3D scene is a stylized small park with three 'walkable' trees. Leaf textures on alpha-cards.
acceptance_criteria: Plays end-to-end across all rungs. Asset bundle <25MB. Local recognition (bd-spec.4) used for re-encoding when available.

title: Whose Tracks?
description: Mammal tracks observation. Sim: a 3D ground scene with simulated tracks; tap to ID. Field: find any animal sign outdoors (track, scat, fur, scratch).
design: Reuse Bird Hour's authoring patterns. Tracks are 2D decals on a 3D ground plane. Self-reported ID is the primary re-encoding path; photo optional.
acceptance_criteria: Plays end-to-end across all rungs. Asset bundle <25MB.

title: Per-rung playthrough harness
description: Headless harness that boots a scenario for each declared (rung, mode) combination and asserts the loop completes end-to-end. Catches age-rung-specific regressions.
design: Playwright + a deterministic test harness. Mock camera, GPS, and network. Capture screenshots per rung for visual review.
acceptance_criteria: `npm run test:scenarios` runs all five MVP scenarios across all declared (rung, mode) combinations and produces a report.

title: Asset compression pipeline
description: Build script that takes raw glTF, audio, images and produces compressed shippable assets: Draco mesh + KTX2 textures, Opus audio, AVIF/WebP images.
design: Use gltfpack and gltf-transform for glTF; basisu for KTX2; ffmpeg for Opus; sharp for AVIF/WebP. Wrapped in a single `npm run build:scenario <slug>` command.
acceptance_criteria: A 50MB raw scenario bundle compresses to <15MB shippable, passes visual diff at acceptable thresholds, plays back identically.

title: Locality-aware scene parameters
description: At scenario load, look up player locality (climate zone, season, sunrise/sunset, current daypart) and pass them as scene parameters. Different lighting / fauna / phenology per locality and time.
design: Locality service: input lat/lon, output { climate_zone, season, daypart, sunrise, sunset, koppen_class }. Internal lookup table for climate zones; SunCalc for sun timings.
acceptance_criteria: Backyard Bird Hour played at 9am in Pasadena vs 5pm in Boston shows different scene lighting, different default bird species lists, different narrator framings.

title: First regional PMTiles extract
description: Build a tippecanoe + go-pmtiles pipeline that produces a Greater LA Area regional pack (target <100MB) from current OSM extracts.
design: Use Geofabrik's California extract, clip to the Greater LA bbox, run tippecanoe with a basemap-friendly schema, convert to PMTiles. Build script is part of the repo.
acceptance_criteria: `npm run build:region la-greater` produces a versioned .pmtiles file <100MB and metadata JSON.

title: MapLibre GL JS + PMTiles integration
description: MapLibre rendering a PMTiles archive served from OPFS. The 'where am I' map shown briefly during onboarding and used by scenarios that opt in.
design: PMTiles via the official @protomaps/basemaps + pmtiles JS package. Style: light theme, kid-friendly. The map is a contextual element; not the primary surface.
acceptance_criteria: A 50MB PMTiles archive of LA County loads from OPFS and renders smoothly at zoom 8-14 on a mid-range Android tablet.

title: Locality and maps
description: MapLibre GL + PMTiles for offline regional maps; locality-aware scene parameters (season, daypart); weather lookup with offline cache.

title: Indoor fallback handling
description: When weather, time, or attention forces an indoor reroute, the manifest's fallback_indoor narrative node runs and the loop continues without scolding. Failure-mode UX is generous.
design: Triggers: parent override, weather check, 30-min timeout, 3+ failed attempts in a row. Re-encoding accepts a self-reported observation in lieu of a photo.
acceptance_criteria: A scenario completed entirely indoors via fallback still produces a re-encoded result and unlocks the next scenario.

title: GPS bounding for safety
description: Scenario-declared max_distance_from_origin_m enforced by Geolocation watchPosition. Soft warn parent at 80% of bound; pause activity at bound. GPS coords obscured to 100m before any storage.
design: watchPosition with adaptive accuracy (lower power when stationary). Warning UX is parent-track only; the child sees a friendly 'time to head back' nudge from the narrator.
acceptance_criteria: With a 50m bound, a tester walking 60m from origin gets a parent warning and a child-track narrator nudge within 5 seconds.

title: Scenario lifecycle (load / save / unlock)
description: The chain that loads a scenario from a content pack, saves player progress, and unlocks the next scenario. Free vs subscriber gating is part of this layer.
design: Scenario state in Dexie keyed by (scenarioId, playerProfileId). Unlock chain declared in manifest's reflection.unlocks.next_scenario. Tier gating (free/digital/hybrid) checked at load.
acceptance_criteria: Window Watch completion auto-unlocks Backyard Bird Hour. Hybrid-tier scenarios are gated for non-hybrid subscribers with a clear (non-pushy) upgrade path.

title: Mentor + apprentice seat config
description: Two-seat UI: parent overlay during sim and field, coaching prompts on the parent's track only, photo-gate handoff. Solo mode is a degenerate two-seat with the parent seat hidden.
design: One device or two devices on the same Wi-Fi (LAN peer for v1.1; v1 single-device co-play). Parent overlay is a slide-out panel toggled by long-press of a corner badge.
acceptance_criteria: Backyard Bird Hour played in mentor+apprentice mode shows parent coaching at the right beats, and the photo-gate prompt is parent-confirmable.

title: Skill rung resolver
description: Resolves which content variant (5-6, 7-8, 9-10, 11-12) applies based on the active player profile, then patches the manifest with skill_rung_overrides. Reading-level-aware UI mode switching.
design: Pure function: (manifest, profile) => resolved-manifest. UI mode (pre_reader / early_reader / fluent_simple / fluent) is part of the resolved output and drives a top-level CSS class.
acceptance_criteria: Same manifest renders three demonstrably different experiences for ages 6, 9, and 11 with no manifest edit.

title: PixiJS 2D overlay
description: PixiJS layered above the 3D canvas for HUD, narrative overlays, tap-to-learn fact cards. Coordinate input routing between 2D and 3D layers.
design: PixiJS in a transparent canvas overlay. Pointer events: 2D first, fallthrough to 3D if no 2D hit. Coordinate space: PixiJS uses screen pixels; pass through R3F's useThree() projection for any 2D-to-3D mapping.
acceptance_criteria: A fact card overlay can be opened from a 3D object tap, dismissed by tapping outside, and renders crisply on a high-DPI tablet.

title: Capacitor wrappers (iOS, Android)
description: Wrap the PWA in Capacitor for App Store + Play distribution. Native bridges for camera, geolocation, file system. Kids Category / Designed for Families config templates.
design: Capacitor 6+. Use Capacitor's official Camera and Geolocation plugins; do not roll your own. Defer push notifications.
acceptance_criteria: `npx cap run ios` produces a runnable iOS build. `npx cap run android` produces an Android APK. Camera and GPS permissions work via native dialog.

title: PhotoGateModal tests are flaky
description: app/components/PhotoGateModal.test.tsx fails intermittently (1-2 runs in 5) on the 'shows the pending photo and confirms via the parent action' and 'discards via the parent action and purges the blob' tests. Failure: confirmedSpy not called within timeout, or blob still present after Discard. Reproduces on main pre-bd-spec.1. Likely a userEvent timing or fake-indexeddb interaction issue. Investigate test isolation / awaitable flush points before they cause CI noise.
close_reason: Root cause: userEvent.click only awaits the synchronous part of the click event; the modal's onClick handler is async (await gate.confirm/discard). Under heavy suite load the post-click expects ran before the awaited gate work completed. Fixed in 03fd1bd by switching post-click expects to waitFor() in app/components/PhotoGateModal.test.tsx (both the 'confirms' and 'discards' tests). Verified by running the full suite back-to-back with the bd-auth.1 changes — 208/208 stable.

title: Wire region-pack checks into biotope-validate CLI
description: docs/region-pack-format.md §9 specifies validator hooks (schema-version sanity, stats consistency, asset coverage, license audit, orphan detection). The functions belong in engine/region-pack/validate.ts and should be reused by tools/biotope-validate.ts via a new --region-pack flag, plus called from the bd-spec.2 build script before writing the pack.

title: Local on-device species recognition (light)
description: A lightweight image classifier that returns 'is this a bird? a plant? a mammal? a reptile? unknown?' with a confidence — NOT full species ID. Used for re-encoding low-bar acceptance.
design: Tiny TF.js or ONNX model, target <5MB. Fine-tuned on iNat coarse labels (kingdom/class). Run in a worker, not the main thread.
acceptance_criteria: Coarse classification of a typical phone photo runs in <500ms on a 2022 mid-range Android tablet with >85% accuracy on bird vs plant vs mammal vs reptile.

title: Online iNaturalist fallback
description: When online and a player taps a species not in their region pack, query the iNaturalist API and surface the result with a 'not in your area' indicator. Rate-limited per the iNat docs.
design: iNat API at api.inaturalist.org/v1/. Rate cap: 60 req/min per app. Cache results to Dexie; never make a request the user didn't initiate.
acceptance_criteria: With network on, a query for an out-of-region species returns within 2s and is cached. With network off, the user gets a friendly 'we'll learn this one when we're online' message.

title: Weather lookup integration
description: Weather check before suggesting a field activity. Online via Open-Meteo (free, no key); offline-cached snapshot used when network is absent.
design: Open-Meteo API. Cache the last 48h forecast on each successful fetch. Map weather codes to manifest weather_unsuitable categories.
acceptance_criteria: With network on, current weather is fetched in <1s. With network off, the most recent cached forecast is used; if too stale (>24h), default to 'safe to go outside' with a parent advisory.

title: Rapier physics (light)
description: Rapier 3D via @react-three/rapier for the very small amount of physics needed in MVP scenarios (e.g., a beetle hotel item dropping into place). SIMD variant on capable browsers.
design: Most MVP scenarios are observation-driven, not physics-driven. Configure Rapier minimally; expand only when a scenario explicitly needs it.
acceptance_criteria: Rapier loaded; a single demo scene with rigid bodies works at 60fps. Scenarios without physics do NOT load Rapier (lazy import).
